chapter_context.summary: Delivers a devastating indictment against Israel's political rulers who devour the people like meat in a pot, attacks mercenary prophets who preach peace when fed but war when not, and prophesies the absolute destruction of Jerusalem ('Zion shall be plowed as a field').

outline.text: Judgment on Corrupt Civil Leaders: Hating the Good, Loving the Evil, and Flaying the People's Skin
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Judgment on Corrupt Civil Leaders: Hating the Good, Loving the Evil, and Flaying the People's Skin (3:1-4), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Micah.

outline.text: Denunciation of Mercenary Prophets: Contrast with Micah, Who is Full of Power by the Spirit of the Lord
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Denunciation of Mercenary Prophets: Contrast with Micah, Who is Full of Power by the Spirit of the Lord (3:5-8), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Micah.

outline.text: The Culminating Doom: Building Zion with Blood and the Prophecy of Jerusalem Becoming Heaps
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Culminating Doom: Building Zion with Blood and the Prophecy of Jerusalem Becoming Heaps (3:9-12), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Micah.

key_themes.title: True Worship as Ethical Integrity
key_themes.description: Micah clarifies that religious rituals are meaningless without ethical behavior in everyday commercial, legal, and social interactions.

key_themes.title: Messianic Hope and Bethlehem
key_themes.description: The book introduces the eternal ruler coming from Bethlehem, pointing to the ultimate messianic restoration and security of God's sheep.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and requirements in Chapter 3, what does it practically mean for you to walk humbly with God in your current season of life?
How does the final vision of a compassionate God casting our sins into the deep sea inspire your daily worship and forgiveness of others?

cultural_historical.title: The Southern Kingdom under Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah
cultural_historical.text: Micah's ministry spanned the reigns of three kings of Judah, representing a period of political tension with Assyria, extreme corruption of the elite classes in Jerusalem, and eventually the reforming reign of Hezekiah.
